reviews.review_msg: If you are in business, do yourself a favour and take BUS 232. Brutal exams and this course takes away your will to live (I promise). Listen to the comments and DO NOT take this course with Schwartz, maybe someone else? but DEFINITELY in person never online, ever.
Guys, if you don't know how to integrate then you definitely shouldn't take this course.. there's double integration

I read the comments but still took the class because I had hope I could pass, boy was I wrong. The midterm average was a fail and the TA's gave horrible feedback. His lectures explain nothing and his assignments are impossible to do since nothing is properly taught. I CANNOT stress this enough, take this course with another prof!
should've listened to the comments

Prof Swartz makes students buy his own textbook (which is actually the worst textbook I've ever had to read) in order to complete assignments(20%). He also doesn't post any lec vids, he posts 2-3 vids that solve the most basic questions you'll encounter. Two exams worth 40% each. His midterm was crazy hard and he doesn't allow qs during testing....

Do not take a class with Schwartz if you don't absolutely have to. I needed a C- in this class and he gave me a D. All lectures are recorded and are around 6-8 minutes long PER WEEK. Otherwise, you are essentially teaching yourself the course via the textbook. What is the point having a prof if they make you teach yourself the course??

Online class not graded on much. Mt1 was fairish but included content we hadn't learned (marks given back, but time wasted). No "real" online lessons, videos show 1 question each. INCREDIBLY difficult final meant to spread out the class' percentages. Dude is shady asf, no avg released but I suspect it was around 20%, I got 63% and got an A+ LOOOOL.
Horrible class. Lecture is heavy on material and he does nothing to help. You basically pay them to then learn all of the material all by yourself.

He's getting paid just to repost his old videos from covid classes and to sell copies of his textbook. He does not care and does nothing to help students. He is lazy and this course is just a source of passive income for him.

Yeah so this dude doesn't care about students learning. He wants the grades to take a specific shape and as a stats prof he'll give whatever he wants in exams. There's no reason for students to do long calculator operations to get the mean, std deviation etc but when u want a low average cuz the midterm was too easy you have to do such things.
I want to kms

Online lectures are 10 minute example problems without context, you have to read the textbook, which assumes you already know the content he's supposed to teach. Final was unfair: tedious work like calculating the mean and stddev of 40 data points before using them in a formula. Means are easy, just give us them so we can finish the problem.

lecture videos are from COVID time. he spends so much of them rambling and complaining about his own slides. you finish the video and are even more confused. the textbook is your best friend. the exams are so weirdly worded. even if you know the material, the questions will make no sense. homework is the only easy thing.

Please don't take this class. Everything is self-taught and it's so complicated. His weekly "lectures" are about 5 minutes long and all he talks about is his soccer jersey. You have to teach yourself through the whole course and it's super hard to get help. If you have to take STAT 203, don't take it with Tim. Take it in person with another prof.
I WANT A REFUND. I PAY SO MUCH MONEY JUST HAVING TO TEACH MYSELF WHAT A PVALUE IS. SFU IS HORRIBLE.

The worst professor ever.  I have made so many nasty reviews but SFU has decided to take them down and not take responsibility for hiring such a horrible teacher. I rather starve myself than ever take this class. My future relies on the education that is given by SFU but this Tim guy does not help with that.

I guess my review got taken down but please if you love yourself don't take it with Tim. Ruined my GPA, my happiness, and my will to continue with school. Lectures don't teach you anything, and you teach yourself. The prof does not even have office hours and did not show up for our midterm.
I never considered suicide until taking his course

Online "lectures" are just 5 - 10 minute example problems with no proper explanation of what we are actually learning. You will need to learn everything from the textbook that he wrote. Unfortunately, the textbook is just his plagiarized notes. For actual learning, I recommend reading Devore's textbook. You will need to be fully self-taught.

P2
The fact that there's a part 2 is insane. This teacher does not care at all about your grade. He will dump work and let the TA do everything so he can sip a margarita and watch football. Idk if it's cuz he's close to retiring but his lack of care is gonna tank your GPA. Your main ally is the textbook, however, he also wrote it so gg have fun.

P1
You know some people apply to jobs and just copy and paste the same applications to 10 jobs. So you could be reading applications about someone looking to be a Starbucks barista while you're hiring for a warehouse worker. This is a summarization of Tim. He could care less about teaching he made lecture videos 7 years ago that are out dated.

This prof is the worst one I have ever had. He's too lazy to teach for more than 5 minutes in his lecture videos and does not give out his email to contact him. This guy needs to be fired asap because the future of education is going to fail because of this guy.

Do not take a course with this prof. Lectures are just 8-10 min videos that summarize the chapter, leaving you to read the textbook and pretty much learn everything by yourself. Marks exams in a way designed to make you do bad, especially the final. Your GPA will take a huge hit. Avoid at all costs.

A completely automatic class with him only sending a long lecture video. He sent out short summary video of the topic to students and it is definitely worth watching this instead of the long lecture video. Make good use of the questions in the online textbook and having a good TA is the key to survive in this course.

His lectures are 8 minutes long and only briefly skim a few topics from each chapter of the textbook, which is 800 pages. Final is all multiple choice but has a grading scheme so that on top of not getting marks for incorrect answers marks are deducted from your correct answers for each wrong one. Class is complete bs. Do NOT take this course
Completely automated class do not take it with him if you dont have to, doesnt have office hours and writes awful exams, doesnt let you see your mark or where you went wrong

Tim has the most boring lectures, the midterms are worth 15% each where you get 15 MCQs that are worth 1% of your grade each. He wont release the questions because he's lazy and wants to reuse them for the finals so you cant even see where you went wrong and try to learn from it. Worst prof i've ever had. do not take

the content isn't too difficult, its the way he tests his students on it. He barely teaches anything on the lectures he uploads and the zoom sessions but his exam questions are so complex and you have little time to work on it. The practice problems and homeworks also do not reflect the type of difficulty the questions on the exams have. avoid prof

The content is actually not hard, but he makes it way more difficult than needed. He aims to have an avg. of 50% for each midterm, so don't be surprised if you fall on the lower end of that spectrum. Especially because his lecture videos are 10 mins long and he just talks about his Tshirt... Take him if you have absolutely no other option.

Feels like a robot presenting the material in his live and recorded lectures. His talking style is very dry, he seems tired and not in the mood to teach the majority of the time, and overall an unhelpful professor.

Assignments are easy. TAs are helpful. But the final are all multiple choice questions, with 8 choices for each question. Some choices are ambiguous and the questions are totally different from the assignment and practice exams. Taking his distance course should consider twice before make the decision, because all you can do is read the textbook.

Never saw the Professor lol. The class had 3 assignments, each worth 10%, and the final was 70%. Assignments were easy, they were all in R and straight out of the textbook. Cheat sheet was allowed for the final and the questions were mostly from the textbook. So make sure to practice ALL the questions , gotta know a bit about simple R code too.
Really nice person to talk to! But the exams are supper hard

I'd say Tim teaches well in class. But still, the whole course is a mess. No assignments or homework are left(also Tim said that he hated the textbook) so that you entirely rely on your own lecture notes. Exams are SUPER SUPER HARD especially when you are left no chance to practice. The grading is also all or nothing. Average is around 40%.

Do not take this course online. You are not allowed to contact the prof and you have to go through the TA if you have any concerns. Expect to teach yourself purely from the textbook, no other course materials. No lecture slides or notes.

Avoid at all costs. Dodges in class questions. Textbook is basically a book of lecture notes, riddled with errors and typos. End of chapter exercises (about 30) only have 2-4 solutions. Questions aren't taken up or solutions provided. Leaves you feeling aimless in an already difficult course. TAs grade all or nothing, no part marks. Equally useless
Terrible, do not take stats 203 online, little help and worst text book ever! The text is your only resource to teach yourself the material, I had to hire a tutor!

didn't have him as a professor, but was forced to use his textbook (there's a 3-page pdf online that lists all the errors in book, most of them numerical errors in examples given) his textbook sucks (confusing and unprofessional explanations) and the only practice midterms he provides are handwritten with spelling mistakes

do NOT take distance ed with him! there are no lecture slides/videos. you will basically be teaching yourself the entire course. he will never provide feedback. its like hes not even the teacher, the TA handles everything. tests are unfair, so much harder than assignments. biggest regret was taking this course!
He is nice but the midterms are really not easy. Formula sheet is allowed in the final. Some problems in final just the same as that in midterm.

Horrible prof regardless of how you take the class with him.  Do not take distance ed with him, because he provides little feedback, does not return midterm or give you feedback on midterm and uses his own textbook which is completely useless.  Impossible to understand material from textbook, and has almost no solutions to problems.

treats distance ed students like nothing. didnt show his face once and didnt offer ANY help periods. TA was incharge of everything and was free 2 hrs out of the whole semester. dont take distance with him.

I don't know if he recently lowered the difficulty level of the quizzes but i find them extremely easy and predictable. I have scored 12/12 on all three quizzes so far and he is very humourous which makes his lectures very enjoyable. Nice professor overall.

Good prof overall, very nice, very helpful, his weakness is testing, Way too hard. For all 4 quizzes average was always around 30-40% (stat 270), Even though hes a nice guy, I wouldn't take him again, because the testing is just a pain in the ass.

A great prof, and the mini-tests are more helpful than assignments. Not worth too much, but keeps you on track. Going to lectures definitely helps, but you must be attentive. His minitests are also very predictable, as he gives us hints in class.

ya.the quizzes arelike mini tests..they take almost the whole hour if you havent practiced problems..ur SCREWED even if u paid attention in class..u gotta work hard..dont take this to boost ur GPA.. its going to bring it down!
The prof teaches slowly and gives lots of examples, BUT his quizzes and exams are EXTREMELY hard.
No Comments
